statement: BAustMS imposes a 12-page limit on submissions; papers exceeding this length are not usually accepted.
BAustMS abstracts must not exceed 200 words.
BAustMS uses MSC 2020 classification with a single primary code and one or more secondary codes.
BAustMS accepts submissions only through the OJS portal at journal.austms.org.au/ojs/ (not ScholarOne).
BAustMS submissions are uploaded as PDF for review; the journal class file (baustms.cls) and bibliography style (srtnumbered.bst) are required only at the post-acceptance source-bundle stage.
The official baustms.cls file is hosted at archive.austms.org.au/Publ/Bulletin/baustms.cls.
note: URL pattern is correct per the BAustMS author instructions, but the archive host was unreachable (TCP timeout) on the fetch attempt. amsart fallback documented in verification.json and ledger.json.

statement: ORCID identifiers are required for all authors in the BAustMS / CUP submission metadata.

Cambridge University Press requires that any use of an artificial-intelligence tool to generate text or images or analyse data for a submission must be appropriately acknowledged and described, and that AI tools are not listed as authors and bear no responsibility for the work.
Cambridge University Press journals may accept pseudonymous authorship at editorial discretion, provided the author fulfils the standard responsibilities of authorship.
BAustMS bibliographic references are listed alphabetically by first-author surname, cited numerically in text, and use AMS journal-abbreviation conventions.
BAustMS reports a typical first-decision turnaround of approximately one week.
BAustMS offers Green Open Access by default and Gold Open Access as an optional paid alternative.
BAustMS does not explicitly require institutional affiliation; independent researchers may submit and use 'Independent Researcher' style affiliations.
Zenodo deposits 10.5281/zenodo.20173677 (concept) and 10.5281/zenodo.20173678 (version 1.0) for the Khinchin-signature PSLQ reproducibility bundle are publicly resolvable.
